Notice Of Motion To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Nov-06-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. PETER N. HADIARIS, TEL. NO. 785-3477 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT PETER HADIARIS' Motion To Compel Arbitration is Granted. The unambiguous language in the Attorney Client Agreement provides that malpractice disputes are subject to binding arbitration. Defendant has not waived his right to enforce the arbitration clause. Plaintiff has not established that the Agreement or enforcement of the arbitration clause is unconscionable. Plaintiff was notified of General Conditions at the time she executed the Agreement. (See Agreement, 4, "This agreement is subject to the general provisions which appear on the reverse or accompany this agreement.") Plaintiff was informed of Defendant's intent to arbitrate by his initial Case Management Statement. (See Case Management Statement, filed July 31, 2013, 15.) Plaintiff's objection to new matters raised in Defendant's reply is overruled. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/EHG)
